47.9,2.0.ch:
Chapter VIII
The Fight in the Desert
47.9,2.0.p: It seemed to Tarzan that he had not closed his eyes before he was awakened, and in another hour the party was on its way south toward Bou Saada.

47.9,2.0.ch:
Chapter VIII
The Fight in the Desert
47.9,2.0.p: Then he turned his horse’s head and rode slowly back to Bou Saada. The front of the Hotel du Petit Sahara, where Tarzan stopped in Bou Saada, is taken up with the bar, two dining-rooms, and the kitchens. Both of the dining-rooms open directly off the bar, and one of them is reserved for the use of the officers of the garrison.
“I shall tell him as soon as he returns.” Tarzan let the officer think that he had been lost, wandering finally into the _douar_ of Kadour ben Saden, who had escorted him back to Bou Saada.
